commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the real behavior proof parser and its focused test to ignore Markdown headings inside fenced terminal output.

PR surface: Tests +25, Other +15. Total +40 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main clearly slices at heading-looking lines without fence awareness, and the remaining PR defect is reproducible from source with a longer outer backtick fence containing a shorter inner fence before `## ...`.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can update the fence helper and regression coverage on this PR branch without a product decision.

target.title: fix(ci): ignore fenced headings in proof parser
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens the Real behavior proof parser so Markdown-looking headings inside fenced terminal output do not end the proof section early.

This keeps the existing proof field requirements and mock-only checks intact; the only change is that section slicing now ignores headings while inside backtick or tilde fences.

## Context

PR #87341 exposed the bug: its real behavior proof copied a generated prompt that contained `## TOOLS.md` inside a fenced output block. The current parser treated that line as the next PR-body heading and missed the later `Observed result after fix` / `What was not tested` fields.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Real behavior proof sections keep parseable fields after copied terminal output that contains Markdown headings such as `## TOOLS.md`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/proof-parser-fence-aware-section`, commit `48cb532a57`, Node 24-compatible runtime.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a direct local node smoke against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs` with a synthetic external PR body whose fenced terminal output contains `## TOOLS.md`, followed by `Observed result after fix` and `What was not tested`. Also ran the focused policy test file with the repo Vitest wrapper.

Evidence after fix: The direct node smoke returned `section contains fenced heading: true`, `status: passed`, and preserved both required fields after the fenced output. Focused regression tests also passed 1 file / 27 tests. Copied output is included below.

Observed result after fix: `extractRealBehaviorProofSections` preserves the fenced `## TOOLS.md` line as proof-section content, and `evaluateRealBehaviorProof` still returns `passed` while reading the required fields that appear after the fenced output.

What was not tested: Live GitHub Actions proof-gate rerun before opening this draft PR. `pnpm test test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts` was attempted locally but pnpm stopped before tests with a dependency purge/install prompt, so the repo node wrapper was used instead.

Direct parser smoke output:

```text
section contains fenced heading: true
status: passed
observedResult: Parser preserved the fenced heading and still read fields below the terminal transcript.
notTested: Live GitHub Actions rerun before opening this draft PR.
```

## Verification

- `node --input-type=module` direct parser smoke against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Refs #87341
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
